title: The Blockchain Transaction Life Cycle
description: A flowchart illustrating the sequential steps a transaction undergoes from initiation to being permanently recorded on the blockchain ledger.
relationships: Transaction is digitally signed with a private key
Signed transaction is broadcast to the P2P network
Network nodes validate transaction integrity and sender's funds
Valid transactions are gathered by miners/validators into a candidate block
Candidate block is propagated to the network
Network reaches consensus on the block's validity (e.g., Proof-of-Work)
Validated block is appended to the blockchain
Subsequent blocks provide confirmations, finalizing the transaction
